title: rhaphiostylis beninensis
intro: Rhaphiostylis beninensis is a plant species commonly known as the African quinine or Benin quinine tree, native to tropical Africa. The medicinal properties of Rhaphiostylis beninensis include its ability to treat fever, malaria, and other tropical diseases, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of consuming Rhaphiostylis beninensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and support immune function. Traditional herbal preparations of Rhaphiostylis beninensis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
